segments: Wealth, Corporate, Commercial and Institutional Banking

supply_chain.dependencies: The Company relies on third-party service providers for certain operations, including technology and operational services.
supply_chain.risks: Failures or disruptions in or breaches of operational, technology or security systems or infrastructure of the Company or third parties, including as a result of cybersecurity incidents. Supply chain disruptions mentioned as a risk factor.

regulatory_risks: Changes to statutes, regulations, or regulatory policies or practices, including capital and liquidity requirements
Changes in trade policy, including the imposition of tariffs or the impacts of retaliatory tariffs
Uncertainty regarding the content, timing and impact of changes to regulatory capital, liquidity and resolution-related requirements applicable to large banking organizations
Actions taken by governmental agencies to stabilize the financial system
Increased Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) assessments, including due to bank failures
Risks related to originating and selling mortgages, including repurchase and indemnity demands
Impacts of current, pending or future litigation and governmental proceedings
Breaches in data security and failures to safeguard personal information
Effects of changes in or interpretations of tax laws and regulations
Consumer protection laws and regulations enforced by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and sanctions laws compliance
Community Reinvestment Act (CRA) requirements
Regulation of brokerage, investment advisory and insurance activities by SEC, FINRA, and state regulators
Regulation of derivatives and swaps marketplace under Dodd-Frank Act
Volcker Rule prohibiting proprietary trading and certain hedge fund/private equity fund interests
Privacy and data protection laws including GLBA, CCPA, GDPR, and state privacy laws
Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) and related consumer reporting regulations
Cybersecurity incident notification and disclosure requirements
Environmental, social and governance (ESG) matters including climate-related financial risk management principles
State 'anti-ESG' measures that may conflict with other regulatory requirements
Basel III Endgame proposed rule implementing stricter capital requirements
Long-term debt requirements proposed for Category III banking organizations
Resolution planning requirements by Federal Reserve and FDIC
Recovery planning requirements by OCC for large national banks
Enhanced prudential standards under Dodd-Frank Act for large banking organizations
Stress testing requirements under CCAR and Dodd-Frank Act
Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) and Net Stable Funding Ratio (NSFR) requirements

sanction_risks: Restrictions on transactions with persons, companies, or foreign governments sanctioned by United States authorities under International Emergency Economic Powers Act and Trading with the Enemy Act
Compliance with Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctions laws
